meta.title: Fonlok — Secure Escrow Payments for Cameroon
meta.description: Fonlok holds your money until both buyer and seller are satisfied. Pay and get paid safely with MTN Mobile Money and Orange Money. No more scams.

hero.description: Fonlok holds the money until both buyer and seller are satisfied. Stop losing money to online scams. Start every deal with confidence.

free.label: Account
free.detail: Create your account and start using Fonlok at no cost. No credit card, no setup fee — just sign up and go.

fee.label: Seller fee
fee.detail: Sellers pay a flat 3% only when a transaction completes. Buyers always pay nothing.

momo.label: Mobile Money
momo.detail: Pay and receive via MTN Mobile Money or Orange Money — works on any phone in Cameroon.

howItWorks.heading: Simple. Safe. Straightforward.
howItWorks.subheading: Three steps is all it takes to do a safe deal on Fonlok.

1.title: Seller creates an invoice
1.desc: Takes 2 minutes. Log in, enter your email, the amount, and a description of what you are selling. Fonlok generates a unique payment link instantly.

2.title: Buyer pays securely
2.desc: Share the payment link with your client. They open it and pay using MTN Mobile Money or Orange Money. Fonlok holds the funds safely in escrow.

3.title: Both sides confirm
3.desc: Deliver what was agreed. The buyer confirms receipt and Fonlok releases the money straight to the seller. Everyone wins.

benefits.heading: Why people choose Fonlok
benefits.subheading: Built specifically for the way people pay and sell in Cameroon.

buyerProtection.title: Buyer protection
buyerProtection.desc: Your money is held until you confirm you received exactly what you paid for. You are never at risk.

sellerGuarantee.title: Seller guarantee
sellerGuarantee.desc: Once you deliver, you get paid. No more chasing unpaid invoices. No more broken promises.

disputeResolution.title: Dispute resolution
disputeResolution.desc: If there is a problem, our team reviews the evidence and resolves it fairly. Neither side can run.

mobileMoney.title: Mobile Money payments

noHiddenCharges.title: No hidden charges

fullyOnline.title: Fully online

pricing.heading: One simple fee

meta.title: How Fonlok Works — Secure Escrow Step by Step
meta.description: Learn how Fonlok protects both buyers and sellers in Cameroon. A step-by-step guide to creating invoices, paying safely, and releasing funds.

hero.description: A simple, five-step process that protects both the buyer and the seller — from the first invoice to the final payout.

1.title: Create an invoice
1.desc: Log in to Fonlok, enter your email address, the agreed amount in XAF, and a short description of what you are selling. The invoice and a unique payment link are created instantly.

2.title: Share the payment link
2.desc: Copy the unique payment link Fonlok generated for your invoice and send it to your buyer by WhatsApp, SMS, or any messaging app. No account needed on their end.

3.title: Pay with Mobile Money
3.desc: The buyer opens the link and pays using MTN Mobile Money or Orange Money. The money goes into Fonlok's secure escrow account — not directly to the seller yet.

4.title: Deliver the goods or service
4.desc: Once Fonlok confirms the payment, you are notified. You then deliver whatever you agreed to — physical goods, a service, digital files, or anything else.

5.title: Confirm and receive payment
5.desc: The buyer marks the order as received. Fonlok immediately releases the money to the seller's account, minus the 3% service fee. Done.

0.q: What if the buyer never confirms delivery?
0.a: If the buyer does not confirm within the agreed window, Fonlok sends a reminder. If there is still no response, our team reviews the evidence and steps in to resolve it fairly.

1.q: What if there is a dispute?
1.a: Either party can open a dispute from their dashboard. Fonlok freezes the funds and asks both sides for evidence. Our team reviews and makes a final decision — funds are either released to the seller or refunded to the buyer.

2.q: What if the seller does not deliver?
2.a: The buyer can open a dispute. If the seller cannot provide proof of delivery, Fonlok refunds the buyer, but in this case, the buyer pays the service fee of 3% from the refunded amount.

3.q: Is the money safe while it is in escrow?
3.a: Yes. Funds held by Fonlok are ring-fenced and cannot be accessed by the seller until delivery is confirmed. They also cannot be accessed by Fonlok for any other purpose.

cta.heading: Ready to try it?
cta.description: Creating an account is free and takes less than two minutes.

meta.title: FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions | Fonlok
meta.description: Answers to common questions about Fonlok — how escrow works, fees, payments, disputes, security, and more.

hero.description: Have a question not listed here?

0.a: Escrow is a system where a trusted third party holds money until both sides of a deal are satisfied. The buyer pays in, and the money is only released to the seller once the buyer confirms they received what they paid for.

1.a: The seller creates an invoice on Fonlok and shares a payment link with the buyer. The buyer pays using Mobile Money. Fonlok holds the funds. Once the buyer confirms delivery, Fonlok pays the seller. If there's a problem, Fonlok investigates.

2.a: No. Fonlok is an escrow platform — we hold funds temporarily as a neutral party during a transaction. We are not a bank and do not offer interest or savings products.

3.q: Can I use Fonlok from my phone?
3.a: Yes. Fonlok works on any device with a web browser — smartphones, tablets, and computers.

0.a: Buyers can pay with MTN Mobile Money and Orange Money. Credit/debit cards are not currently supported.

1.q: Who pays the Fonlok fee — buyer or seller?
1.a: The seller pays a 3% fee on each transaction. Buyers pay the exact invoice amount with no additional charges from Fonlok.

2.q: Are there monthly fees or subscription plans?
2.a: No. Fonlok is completely free to sign up for and free to use. The only cost is the 3% fee when a transaction is completed.

3.q: How long does it take to receive my money after delivery is confirmed?
3.a: Fonlok initiates the payout immediately after the buyer confirms delivery. Depending on your mobile network, funds typically arrive within a few minutes to a few hours.

0.q: What if the buyer does not confirm delivery?
0.a: If the buyer does not respond within the expected window, Fonlok will contact both parties to resolve the situation. We review all available evidence before making a final decision.

1.q: What happens if there is a dispute?
1.a: Either party can open a dispute from their Fonlok dashboard. We freeze the funds and request evidence from both sides — photos, messages, delivery confirmations, etc. Our team reviews everything and decides whether to release or refund the money.

2.q: What if the seller does not deliver?
2.a: The buyer can open a dispute. If the seller cannot prove delivery, Fonlok refunds the buyer in full.

3.a: Yes. All transactions are encrypted and funds held in escrow are ring-fenced. We use secure payment processors and never store sensitive payment credentials on our servers.

0.a: Yes. Creating an account on Fonlok is free and takes less than two minutes.

1.q: Do I need an account to pay as a buyer?
1.a: You can make a payment via the invoice link without an account. However, creating a free account gives you access to payment history, dispute management, and order tracking.

2.q: How do I change my password or phone number?
2.a: Log in to your account and go to Settings. From there you can update your phone number and change your password.

meta.title: Pricing — Simple & Transparent | Fonlok
meta.description: One fee, no surprises. Fonlok charges sellers 3% per transaction. Buyers pay nothing. No monthly fees, no setup costs.

hero.description: No monthly plans. No hidden fees. No setup costs. You only pay when you complete a transaction.

seller.description: Paid when the buyer confirms receipt. If a transaction is refunded, you do not pay.

buyer.description: Buyers pay exactly the invoice amount. Fonlok does not charge buyers anything.

features.0: Pay via MTN or Orange Money

networkNote.heading: About Mobile Money network charges
networkNote.body: MTN and Orange apply their own transaction fees on Mobile Money payments (typically around 1–2%). These charges are collected by the mobile network operator at the moment of payment and are completely separate from Fonlok's 3% fee. Fonlok does not receive or control these charges.

referral.heading: Earn back your fee — and more
referral.description: Join the Fonlok referral programme and earn 0.5% commission on every transaction made by the people you refer. Refer enough people and your referral earnings will more than cover your own transaction fees.

cta.heading: Ready to start?
cta.description: Creating an account is free. You only pay when a deal is done.

seller.feeLabel: of each transaction amount — charged to the seller only
seller.body: That 3% covers everything Fonlok does for you: holding the funds, processing the payout, handling disputes, and sending receipts. Your buyer pays zero Fonlok fees — which makes it easier for you to convince them to use the platform.

buyer.body: When you pay through a Fonlok invoice link, you pay exactly the amount shown. Fonlok adds no markup or service charge for buyers.
buyer.noteBody: Standard Mobile Money network charges (typically 2%) applied by MTN or Orange at the point of payment are set by and paid to the mobile network — not collected by Fonlok.

valueSection.heading: What do sellers get for 3%?
valueSection.description: The fee is not just a charge — it is the entire service. Here is exactly what it covers.

0.title: Guaranteed payment before you deliver
0.desc: The buyer's money is locked in escrow before you lift a finger. You only deliver when the funds are confirmed.

1.title: Funds held securely
1.desc: Fonlok holds the money as a neutral party. Neither side can access it until the deal is completed.

2.title: Automatic payout on confirmation
2.desc: Once the buyer confirms delivery, Fonlok releases your money immediately. No chasing, no waiting.

3.title: Payment receipts by email
3.desc: Both buyer and seller receive email receipts automatically. A clear paper trail for every deal.

4.title: Dispute resolution included
4.desc: If something goes wrong, Fonlok investigates and makes a fair decision. You are never left to fight alone.

5.title: Buyers pay zero Fonlok fees
5.desc: Your buyers are not charged anything by Fonlok. That removes their hesitation and makes it easier for you to close deals.

examples.heading: What does 3% look like in practice?
examples.description: The seller pays the fee. Buyers always pay exactly what the invoice says.

0.q: What about the Mobile Money network charge buyers see on their phone?
0.a: When a buyer pays with MTN or Orange Money, the mobile network deducts their own transaction fee (typically around 2%) directly at the point of payment. This is a standard charge applied to all MoMo transactions, not something Fonlok controls or receives. Fonlok collects nothing from buyers.

1.q: Who pays the 3% fee — buyer or seller?
1.a: The seller pays the fee. It is deducted from the amount released to the seller when the deal is confirmed. Buyers always pay the exact invoice amount.

2.q: Are there any monthly or subscription fees?
2.a: No. You only pay when you use Fonlok. Creating an account, creating invoices, and browsing your dashboard are all completely free.

3.a: No. Getting started is completely free.

4.q: What if a deal is cancelled or refunded?
4.a: If no delivery has taken place and the buyer's funds are refunded, the buyer pays for the 3% service fee.
